protocol: full-set blind heuristic-formula, initial scoring pass, cross-question consistency audit, documented family-level revision, and complete final rescoring

isolation_confirmation.statement: Only the protocol prompt and sanitized questions were used. No labels, candidate outcomes, training runs, simulations, or empirical performance reconstruction were consulted.

mlp.assumptions: depth counts constant-width hidden affine layers; every affine and output layer has a bias; each enabled LayerNorm has trainable scale and bias; same-width residual skips add no parameters.

transformer_lm.decomposition: token embedding V*d; learned position embedding C*d; each block has QKV and attention output projections, two FFN affine layers, their biases, and two LayerNorm scale/bias pairs; final LayerNorm; untied biased LM head.
transformer_lm.assumptions: num_heads changes attention partitioning but not projection parameter count at fixed d_model; embeddings and LM head are untied.

bigram_lm.equation: Score = parameter_capacity + learning_rate_fit + optimizer_suitability + architecture_fit + regularization_fit + budget_fit + interaction_terms

features.optimizer_suitability: base by optimizer plus SGD momentum adjustment
features.budget_fit: 0 because all choices have 80 steps, batch 64, and 5120 samples; short-budget risk enters interactions

interaction_terms: P>200000 receives the declared short-budget penalty
bigram_lm.rationale: The data-generating rule is only bigram-scale, so moderate transformers are sufficient; at 80 steps optimizer/lr trainability matters more than maximal depth or width.

multivariate_regression.equation: Score = parameter_capacity + learning_rate_fit + optimizer_suitability + architecture_fit + regularization_fit + budget_fit + interaction_terms

features.optimizer_suitability: adaptive-optimizer prior with momentum credit for SGD
features.architecture_fit: depth fit + width fit + 0.7*smooth_activation_fraction + residual credit - 0.15*layer_norm_fraction
features.budget_fit: 0 because all choices have 160 steps, batch 32, and 5120 samples; scale interactions encode trainability

interaction_terms: SGD lr<0.003: -1.0; SGD lr>=0.01 with momentum: +0.4
multivariate_regression.rationale: The visible noiseless four-variable expression mixes smooth univariate effects and cross-variable periodic products, favoring moderate nonlinear capacity, smooth activations, and optimization that makes progress within 160 steps.

univariate_regression.equation: Score = parameter_capacity + learning_rate_fit + optimizer_suitability + architecture_fit + regularization_fit + budget_fit + interaction_terms

features.optimizer_suitability: adaptive-optimizer prior with momentum credit for SGD
features.architecture_fit: depth fit + width fit + 0.9*smooth_activation_fraction + deep-residual credit - 0.1*layer_norm_fraction
features.budget_fit: 0 because all choices have 64 steps, batch 32, and 2048 samples; short-budget effects enter interactions

interaction_terms: SGD lr<0.003: -1.1; SGD lr>=0.01 with momentum: +0.4
univariate_regression.rationale: The visible target is a noiseless sinusoid plus linear trend. Smooth activations and a compact-to-moderate network are useful, while 64 steps make low learning rates and very large MLPs risky.

anomalies_found.evidence: 45 candidate IDs recur; every recurrence received an identical initial score within its family despite different question letters.

anomalies_found.issue: Bigram depth/capacity risk was slightly underweighted.
anomalies_found.evidence: The initial formula could let a three-layer or >200k-parameter transformer recover too much score from optimizer and learning-rate terms despite only 80 updates on a first-order process.

anomalies_found.issue: Very low learning rates on multivariate regression were insufficiently penalized.
anomalies_found.evidence: At 160 fixed updates, raw capacity could partly offset lr<=1e-4 even though the larger nonlinear model has little time to exploit that capacity.

anomalies_found.issue: Smooth nonlinearities were underrepresented for the two visible trigonometric targets.
anomalies_found.evidence: The formula acknowledged SiLU/GELU but their initial coefficient was small relative to optimizer terms; increasing it reduces implausible preference for piecewise-linear configurations when optimization scores are close.

anomalies_found.issue: Very large univariate MLPs needed a stronger short-budget penalty.
anomalies_found.evidence: The target is one-dimensional and noiseless and the budget is only 64 steps; capacity above 100k parameters is more likely to remain undertrained than to add useful approximation power.

consistency_audit.dominance_check: No single contribution was allowed to determine every choice: final totals retain separate parameter, lr, optimizer, architecture, regularization, budget, and interaction components. Learning-rate shape and optimizer suitability remain distinct; explicit optimizer-lr bonuses are small and used only to model joint trainability.
consistency_audit.double_counting_check: The audit kept base optimizer suitability independent of lr distance, and reserved interaction terms for threshold behavior (extreme lr, scale-budget mismatch, residual-depth coupling). Capacity curvature measures representational scale; the separate large-model interaction measures trainability under the fixed step budget.
proposed_changes: Increase bigram extra-layer and >200k-parameter short-budget penalties.
Increase multivariate lr<=1e-4 penalty and smooth-activation coefficient.
Increase univariate smooth-activation coefficient and >100k-parameter short-budget penalty.
Apply all changes at family level and rescore all questions in each affected family.

formula_revisions.reason: A first-order language process does not justify rewarding depth that is hard to optimize in 80 steps.
Separate representational capacity from the stronger training-time risk of a large transformer under 80 updates.
The 160-step budget makes the smallest visible adaptive learning rates especially unlikely to realize nominal capacity.
The visible target contains tanh, sine, and cosine terms plus smooth interactions, so SiLU/GELU alignment merits more weight.
The visible target is a sinusoid plus linear trend; smooth activations have a direct approximation advantage.
For a noiseless one-dimensional function and 64 steps, very large models carry a pronounced trainability cost.

bigram_lm.equation: Score = parameter_capacity + learning_rate_fit + optimizer_suitability + architecture_fit + regularization_fit + budget_fit + interaction_terms

features.optimizer_suitability: base by optimizer plus SGD momentum adjustment
features.budget_fit: 0 because all choices have 80 steps, batch 64, and 5120 samples; short-budget risk enters interactions

interaction_terms: P>200000 receives the declared short-budget penalty
bigram_lm.rationale: The data-generating rule is only bigram-scale, so moderate transformers are sufficient; at 80 steps optimizer/lr trainability matters more than maximal depth or width.

multivariate_regression.equation: Score = parameter_capacity + learning_rate_fit + optimizer_suitability + architecture_fit + regularization_fit + budget_fit + interaction_terms

features.optimizer_suitability: adaptive-optimizer prior with momentum credit for SGD
features.architecture_fit: depth fit + width fit + 1*smooth_activation_fraction + residual credit - 0.15*layer_norm_fraction
features.budget_fit: 0 because all choices have 160 steps, batch 32, and 5120 samples; scale interactions encode trainability

interaction_terms: SGD lr<0.003: -1.0; SGD lr>=0.01 with momentum: +0.4
multivariate_regression.rationale: The visible noiseless four-variable expression mixes smooth univariate effects and cross-variable periodic products, favoring moderate nonlinear capacity, smooth activations, and optimization that makes progress within 160 steps.

univariate_regression.equation: Score = parameter_capacity + learning_rate_fit + optimizer_suitability + architecture_fit + regularization_fit + budget_fit + interaction_terms

features.optimizer_suitability: adaptive-optimizer prior with momentum credit for SGD
features.architecture_fit: depth fit + width fit + 1.2*smooth_activation_fraction + deep-residual credit - 0.1*layer_norm_fraction
features.budget_fit: 0 because all choices have 64 steps, batch 32, and 2048 samples; short-budget effects enter interactions

interaction_terms: SGD lr<0.003: -1.1; SGD lr>=0.01 with momentum: +0.4
univariate_regression.rationale: The visible target is a noiseless sinusoid plus linear trend. Smooth activations and a compact-to-moderate network are useful, while 64 steps make low learning rates and very large MLPs risky.

predictions.change_reason: No change; the initial winner remains highest after all uniform family-level revisions.
predictions.detailed_decision_trace: Final bigram_lm formula gives c_1c667d 4.8268, ahead of c_2e1fc1 3.773 by 1.0538. Winner contributions: parameter 1.1096, lr 2.4172, optimizer 1.2, architecture 0, regularization -0.25, invariant budget 0, interactions 0.35. Risk flags: strong_weight_decay_underfit_risk. The decision uses only the visible architecture, optimizer, and fixed budget.

main_drivers: Non-monotonic optimizer-specific learning-rate fit under fixed step budgets.
Capacity centered on task complexity rather than monotonic parameter reward.
Optimizer suitability plus narrowly scoped optimizer-lr and scale-budget interactions.
Smooth-activation, residual, normalization, depth, and width structure for the visible regression expressions.
Uniform repeated-candidate scoring across the full set.
uncertainties: The exact implementation details of residual placement, LayerNorm placement, positional embeddings, and tied versus untied LM heads are not visible; parameter arithmetic states explicit assumptions.
Heuristic lr targets are architecture- and initialization-sensitive, especially for RMSprop and Adagrad.
Layer normalization can either stabilize short-budget optimization or suppress useful amplitude information in scalar regression depending on placement.
The scoring rule ranks candidates but is not calibrated to predict test CE or MSE numerically.
final_reflection.label_free_confirmation: All revisions came from visible-spec logical consistency and repeated-candidate auditing, never from labels or observed performance.
